_comment: Machine-readable batch tracker. Update after each session.

blog.cannibalisation_note: security-driver-sao-paulo dropped from Batch 8 — duplicate of /security-drivers/sao-paulo/ service page built in Phase 3C Block 2. Do not blog topics already covered by Phase 3C deep pages.

event-security.note: All 10 priority cities covered. Total event-security silo now 18 cities.

pages._count_note: 350 non-index content .md files + 6 root pages + section indexes (~367 Hugo pages). Reconciled 2026-06-03 from the actual content tree; prior 261 was stale.

engine_alignment.4_internal_link_graph: IN PROGRESS - Stage 2J country-hub block done 2026-06-04 (all 38 country pages prose-linked to cities + service silos; total links 281->395, avg 0.92->1.30). Remaining: blog/event-security/risk-assessment prose-link pass. NOTE: link/QA scripts count only prose body links, not template-rendered front-matter city/service links.
engine_alignment.5_qa_seo_quality_gate: SCRIPTS DONE - Stage 2L error pass complete 2026-06-03 (0 errors, holding). Thin-city FAQ-floor expansion done 2026-06-04; warnings 651->463. Remaining WARNINGS: 4-FAQ cities, prose-link floors, description length

cannibalisation_rule: Do not blog on topics already covered by Phase 3C service x city deep pages. The deep pages ARE the definitive content for bodyguard-hire/{city}, security-drivers/{city}, executive-protection/{city}, residential-security/{city}. Blog articles on these topics should cover angles the deep pages do not: cost breakdowns, regulatory explainers, comparison pieces, question-led safety guides.

thin_city_expansion.note: All cities that were at 3 FAQs expanded to full depth (5 FAQs, 4+ body sections, 2+ prose links) across batches 2-10. Cleared FAQ-floor warnings for those cities.

next_stage: Continue Stage 2J: prose-link pass over blog (many 0-1 link articles), event-security (18) and risk-assessment (18) silos. Optionally lift cities sitting at exactly 4 FAQs to 5. Then duplicate-country-page resolution (uk/united-kingdom, usa/united-states share titles).
